articles.title: CEQ Permitting Innovators Program Call for Solutions
articles.summary: NASA’s Center of Excellence for Collaborative Innovation (CoECI) assists in the use of crowdsourcing across the federal government. CoECI’s NASA Tournament Lab offers the contract capability to run external crowdsourced challenges on behalf of NASA and other agencies. The Council on Environmental Quality (CEQ), within the Executive Office of the President established by the National […]

articles.title: House Appropriators Poised to Reject Proposed NASA Budget Cuts
articles.summary: The House Appropriations committee is poised once again to reject the deep cuts to NASA proposed by the Trump Administration for FY2027. The Commerce-Justice-Science subcommittee is recommending $24.4 billion, the […]

articles.title: Plasma-hot Space Rider tests for belly and flaps
Stunning images from Biomass mark its one year in orbit
articles.summary: To mark the first anniversary of the European Space Agency’s Biomass satellite, we present a selection of striking images captured over the past 12 months, revealing Earth’s forests, and much more, in new detail. In just one year, this pioneering mission has begun transforming our understanding of forest dynamics and advancing how scientists monitor the critical role forests play in regulating the global carbon cycle.

articles.title: A Falcon 9 upper stage will strike the Moon in August
articles.summary: The object will be traveling at 2.43 km a second, or 5,400 mph, upon impact.

articles.title: Fires Rage in Georgia
articles.summary: Firefighters are battling two destructive blazes in the southern part of the state as drought grips the U.S. Southeast.

articles.title: Key Senators Agree NASA FY2027 Budget Request Inadequate
articles.summary: Senate appropriators from both sides of the aisle are joining their House counterparts in opposing President Trump’s proposed 23 percent cut to NASA for FY2027. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman once […]

articles.title: Live coverage: SpaceX seeks second attempt at Falcon Heavy launch following weather scrub on Monday
articles.summary: The mission will send the ViaSat-3 F3 satellite to a geosynchronous transfer orbit. Liftoff from Launch Complex 39A at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center is scheduled for 10:13 a.m. EDT (1413 UTC), at the opening of an 85-minute window.

articles.title: NASA Connects Little Red Dots with Chandra, Webb
articles.summary: A newly discovered object may be a key to unlocking the true nature of a mysterious class of sources that astronomers have found in the early universe in recent years. A “X-ray dot” found by NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory could explain what the hundreds or potentially thousands of these objects are. A paper describing the […]

articles.title: There’s No Place Like NASA’s New X-59 Hangar Home
articles.summary: There’s no sign reading “home sweet home” in the hangar where the X‑59 now sits, but the sentiment is unmistakable among those tending to the quiet supersonic aircraft. Located at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, the X-59 hangar was built in 1968 but looks like new thanks to a full renovation and […]

articles.title: Nighttime Imaging Grows Landsat’s Science Value
articles.summary: By Earth Resources Observation and Science (EROS) Center  For more than 50 years, Landsat has imaged Earth’s land and near-shore surfaces as the satellites descend in midmorning orbit, when daily sunlight is optimal. That’s just what they’ve always done.  Currently, Landsat 8 and Landsat 9 circle the globe while also making better use of their ascending paths, […]

articles.title: Curiosity Captures a 360-Degree View at ‘Nevado Sajama’
articles.summary: Description NASA’s Curiosity Mars rover captured this 360-degree view of a region filled with low ridges called boxwork formations between Nov. 9 and Dec. 7, 2025 (the 4,714th to 4,741st Martian days, or sols, of the mission). At 1.5 billion pixels, this is one of the largest panoramas Curiosity has ever taken (the rover’s largest […]
